expectations.text: Correctly explains the var hoisting / shared closure pitfall (all closures share the same 'i' variable)
expectations.evidence: Lines 6-16: explains var is function-scoped creating a single i, all closures reference it, by call time i===5

expectations.text: Offers fix using let instead of var
expectations.evidence: Lines 24-36: 'Fix 1: Replace var with let' with code example, explains per-iteration binding

expectations.text: Offers a more functional solution using Array.from or map instead of the imperative loop
expectations.evidence: Lines 42-66: 'Fix 2: Use Array Methods (Preferred Functional Style)' with Array.from({ length: 5 }, (_, i) => ...) and forEach/querySelectorAll examples

expectations.text: The functional solution avoids mutation (no push)
expectations.evidence: Array.from example builds the array directly without push. forEach example still uses push but Array.from variant does not. Primary functional example is push-free.
